statement.markdown:
You are given a Max Heap represented as an array. Your task is to max heapify a given sub-array of the heap. The given sub-array is represented by a start index and a count of elements to be max heapified.
Note: This function must be modified to correctly max heapify the sub-array. This function should be called from the main routine (not provided) where the heap and the indices for max heapification are populated.
statement.inputFormat: 1-based index of the start of the sub-array to max heapify (inclusive) and the count of elements in that sub-array
statement.outputFormat: None. The function needs to modify the input array to be a max heap.
statement.notes: Assume that the heap is initially empty and the sub-array elements are all unique.

constraints.description: 1-based index of the start of the sub-array to max heapify (inclusive)
The count of elements in the sub-array to max heapify

examples.explanation: Max heapifying the sub-array at index 1 with 3 elements results in a max heap [1, 2, 3]
Max heapifying the sub-array at index 10 with 4 elements results in a max heap [10, 9, 8, 7]

functionSignature.cpp: bool swapAndHeapify(int arr[], int i, int j, int count);
functionSignature.java: boolean swapAndHeapify(int[] arr, int i, int j, int count);
functionSignature.python: def swapAndHeapify(self, arr, i, j, count: int) -> bool:
functionSignature.javascript: boolean swapAndHeapify(int[] arr, int i, int j, int count);
